hawkish_phrases: core inflation remaining well above the Committee's objective
concern that the Committee's larger policy action could be seen as a premature declaration of victory on the price-stability part of the dual mandate
housing services prices were continuing to rise at an elevated rate
risks that progress toward the Committee's 2 percent inflation objective could be stalled
dovish_phrases: downside risks to employment were seen as having increased
reducing policy restraint too late or too little could risk unduly weakening economic activity and employment
gained greater confidence that inflation is moving sustainably toward 2 percent
job gains had slowed, and the unemployment rate had moved up
further cooling did not appear to be needed to help bring inflation back to 2 percent
summary: The Committee cut rates by 50 basis points to 4.75-5.0% with one dissenter (Bowman) preferring 25bp, citing greater confidence inflation is moving sustainably to 2% and increased downside risks to employment, though inflation remains somewhat elevated.
